description.p: Grist is a modern relational spreadsheet. It combines the flexibility of a spreadsheet with the robustness of a database.
Columns work like they do in databases: they are named, and they hold one kind of data. They can also be filled by formula, spreadsheet-style, with automatic updates when referenced cells change.

ul.li: Python formulas, with many Excel functions available.
A portable, self-contained file format, based on SQlite.
Convenient editing and formatting features, including references, attachments, conditional formatting, and Markdown formatting.
Drag and drop dashboards, with charts, cards, calendars, summary tables, filters, and more.
Import from and export to CSV or Excel files. Supports incremental imports.
Files can be displayed on a static website with grist-static.
The app can also be hosted online, or files can be displayed on a static website.

screenshot.caption: Sample spreadsheet, showing a list of people, and a tutorial window explaining the application
Chemical elements spreadsheet, showing a table, a card, a card list and a chart
